alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs at Google; proven cross-functional execution across engineering, product, and business teams
alignments.job_requirement: Coordinate across technical and non-technical stakeholders through the full agent development lifecycle process

alignments.candidate_evidence: Commercial Operations leadership with responsibility for customer outcomes and revenue recovery; experience translating customer needs into product requirements and execution roadmaps
alignments.job_requirement: Be a trusted advisor to our customers and drive their AI strategies

alignments.candidate_evidence: Managed global transformation programs at scale; demonstrated ability to own complex multi-workstream initiatives and deliver results across distributed teams
alignments.job_requirement: Drive execution and delivery of multiple complex, high-visibility agent development projects

alignments.candidate_evidence: Advanced Excel and Sheets; proficient SQL (joins, GROUP BY, subqueries, window functions); builds Looker dashboards; writes BigQuery queries to pull and shape own data
alignments.job_requirement: Contribute data-driven, strategic insights to customers and internal team decisions

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years in tech operations; designs and directs LLM coding agents; manages Salesforce admin and engineering feature requests; experience with product requirements, UAT, and rollout
alignments.job_requirement: Strong understanding of software development, product management and engineering principles

alignments.candidate_evidence: Professional English fluency; eight years delivering complex technical programs across global stakeholders; positioned as operations and strategy leader
alignments.job_requirement: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to convey complex technical information to non-technical stakeholders

gaps.gap: Product experience is primarily operations, requirements, UAT, and rollout rather than native product management or SDLC depth
gaps.mitigation: Role emphasizes conversational design and agent development strategy over PM methodology; candidate's transformation and execution focus aligns with agent-scoping and customer advisory work. Shadowing and onboarding can bridge PM-specific practices.

gaps.gap: Limited direct consulting or forward-deployed customer strategy experience; unclear depth of direct customer advisory relationships
gaps.mitigation: JD emphasizes trusted advisor role and customer strategy, but does not require prior consulting or FDE background. Candidate's global ops leadership and multi-stakeholder coordination provide adjacent skills; customer-facing mentoring and structured advisory programs can accelerate.

gaps.gap: Technical understanding is broad and systems-level; no prior experience designing or shipping conversational AI agents
gaps.mitigation: Role is strategist and coordinator, not engineer. Candidate's AI/LLM tooling direction and agent evaluation methodology, combined with execution experience, provide a foundation. Product and design immersion during onboarding is standard for this function.

gaps.gap: JD preferred: CS/Engineering degree or MBA; candidate has neither explicitly stated
gaps.mitigation: Degree preference is marked 'strong plus,' not required. Eight years of technical operations leadership at Google and equivalent practical expertise satisfy the equivalence clause.

output.level_rationale: JD emphasizes complex multi-project execution, customer advisory, stakeholder coordination, and strategic decision-making across full development lifecycle. reporting and company context (Sierra founded by Google/Salesforce executives, scaling AI platform) suggest established-stage growth role. No explicit years posted. "Strategist" title at product-stage company with cross-functional scope and trusted-advisor accountability point toward L5 IC or Lead-level equivalent. Confidence is moderate due to ambiguity in exact seniority signaling and lack of posted years requirement.
output.summary: Strategist, Agent Development is a strong role-family fit for a Strategy and Operations leader. Candidate's eight-year track record in global commercial transformation, cross-functional execution, stakeholder coordination, and data-driven decision-making map directly to agent development scoping, customer advisory, and multi-project delivery. Key gaps—native product management depth, direct customer consulting, and prior conversational AI experience—are manageable and typical for someone transitioning into a new product domain at this level. Communication and analytical skills are well-evidenced; technical understanding is broad but not production-engineering focused, which matches the strategist and coordinator scope of the role.
uncertainties: Company size and stage context not stated in JD; no salary posted to triangulate level
Exact reporting line and manager seniority not specified
Unclear how much of the role is internal strategy vs. direct customer engagement depth
No explicit requirement for prior conversational AI or agent-development exposure, but extent of expected fluency during onboarding is unstated
